title: Our Cup Runneth Over

jokes.text: Immigration? - Revenue. Mrs. Rose! There are people here from the government!
jokes.punchline: Reveal it's actually tax/financial trouble
jokes.explanation: Efficient misdirection that immediately establishes the family's financial crisis

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We need to talk about our legal status in this country.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh my god, are we being deported?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, it's worse. The IRS is auditing us.

jokes.text: I've been gutted! John, I've been stripped of every morsel of pleasure I earned in this life!
jokes.setup: Moira's dramatic response to losing wealth
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top theatrical language for a financial setback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira character comedy - theatrical overstatement that reveals her privileged worldview

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm not trying to be dramatic, but I think this might be the end of the world.

jokes.text: Those bags are not for you. My boyfriend bought those for me. So, theoretically, they are his!
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to save her designer bags from seizure
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that somehow makes the bags her boyfriend's property
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's entitled logic and inability to grasp the reality of asset seizure

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so if David bought me the bags, then technically they're his bags, which means they're his property, which means the government can't take them because they belong to my boyfriend.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's... that's not how any of this works.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, David, think about it. If they're your bags, then they're not my assets. Problem solved.

jokes.text: My very soul has been kidnapped. There's no ransom. No one's coming to save me!
jokes.setup: Moira continuing her dramatic response to financial ruin
jokes.punchline: Treating material loss as literal soul kidnapping
jokes.explanation: More Moira theatricality, establishing her tendency to dramatize everything

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: They've taken everything. My Birkins, my baubles, my bespoke collection of avant-garde wigs. Do you understand what this means? They haven't just seized my assets—they've kidnapped my very essence. I am now a shell of a woman, a husk, a footnote in the tragic opera of my own life.

jokes.text: No! Did you put Kristen with Robin? They don't like each other!
jokes.setup: Family losing everything in government seizure
jokes.punchline: Moira worried about seating arrangements of her wigs during a crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Moira treating wigs like people while losing everything else

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: But what about my wigs? Where will they sit during the seizure?

jokes.text: The kids. The children are dependents, Moira.
jokes.setup: Lawyer mentions one asset they can retain
jokes.punchline: Moira's confusion reveals she forgot about her own children
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery revealing Moira's priorities - she literally forgot about her children

dialogue.speaker: Lawyer
dialogue.line: Well, there is one asset you'll be able to retain.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Lawyer
dialogue.line: Your children.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh thank God. For a moment there, I thought you were going to say something I actually cared about.

jokes.text: Yes, I bought that as a joke for my son. Wait, you actually purchased that town? Yes, I purchased the town. How else could I get the deed?
jokes.setup: Reveal that Johnny owns a town
jokes.punchline: Johnny's logic that you have to actually buy something to own it
jokes.explanation: Absurd premise grounded in Johnny's practical but misguided logic

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, you own the town?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, not exactly. I mean, I live here, I work here...

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So you don't own it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, but I've been here so long, I feel like I own it.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's not how ownership works, Dad.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, you have to actually buy something to own it, apparently.

jokes.text: You could've photoshopped the deed! And saved the money! Why would I Photoshop a deed? The joke was owning the town!
jokes.punchline: Johnny's commitment to an expensive joke's authenticity
jokes.explanation: Shows Johnny's wealthy person logic and commitment to expensive gestures

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You could just buy a regular humidifier.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: A regular humidifier? Alexis, a humidifier is a humidifier. It doesn't matter if it costs $40 or $4,000 — it does the same thing. But I'm not going to buy a $40 humidifier because then people will think I bought a $40 humidifier.

jokes.text: Well, homelessness is still on the table.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of homelessness as an option
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly by the lawyer

dialogue.speaker: David Rose
dialogue.line: Well, if this living situation doesn't work out, I suppose homelessness is always an option.

jokes.text: There's like, cows all over the place. Like, everywhere. I don't know if there's an infestation.
jokes.punchline: Treating cows on farms as an 'infestation'
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's complete disconnect from rural life - cows as 'infestation'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: The cows on the farms here are treated like an infestation.

jokes.text: So if you're looking for an ass to kiss, it's mine!
jokes.punchline: Crude way of asserting his authority
jokes.explanation: Establishes Roland's crude, small-town mayor personality

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm Roland Schitt, and I'm the mayor of this town.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: So if you want something done around here, you come see me. And if you don't like it, you can suck it.

jokes.text: Children, keep an eye on these bags. Apparently in hell, there's no bellman!
jokes.punchline: Moira comparing motel service to hell
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - dramatic overstatement about basic service expectations

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This is hell. I'm convinced this is hell. They've just hidden it behind a neon sign and a continental breakfast.

jokes.text: David, what are we... - Shut up! - You shut up! - Um, you shut up! - You shut up!
jokes.punchline: Immediate descent into childish arguing
jokes.explanation: Basic sibling comedy but shows family dynamics under stress

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, we need to focus on the actual problem here.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry, are you the only one allowed to have opinions?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not what I said.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You literally always do this.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I do not 'literally always' do anything.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yes, you do. You're doing it right now.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know what? You're being ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, you smell.

jokes.text: So we cater more to off road truckers and drunk teenagers.
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt description of motel clientele
jokes.explanation: Establishes Stevie's dry humor and the motel's true nature

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, we get a lot of people passing through. Truckers, traveling salesmen, people who need to rent a room by the hour...

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Look, the point is, you're not going to run into the kind of clientele you're used to. These are people who are one step away from living in their car, and some of them are already there.

jokes.text: I just want a bathtub and a long extension cord, please.
jokes.punchline: Implied suicide threat with extension cord
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows Moira's dramatic response to their situation

jokes.text: You know, I did the deed in just about every room in this place.
jokes.punchline: TMI about his sexual history at the motel
jokes.explanation: Typical Roland oversharing - crude but character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, back in my day at the motel, we had a lot of... activity. I mean, a lot of couples would check in for the weekend, and let me tell you, the walls were thin. Very thin. I heard things. Did things. Mostly heard things, but definitely did some things too. My point is, I've seen it all at that motel. And by seen, I mean heard. And by heard, I mean participated in some capacity that I probably shouldn't talk about at a dinner table.

jokes.text: if a forensic team came in here with one of those blue lights, this place would just light up!
jokes.punchline: Graphic visualization of bodily fluids everywhere
jokes.explanation: Escalates the previous joke with visual imagery - gross but effective

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I've had relations in every room of that motel. Some rooms multiple times a day.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, that's... that's a lot of rooms.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, when you factor in all the bodily fluids, it's basically a Jackson Pollock painting in there.

jokes.text: Ew! Ew! Ew! Ew! Ew! - Oh, my God. Oh, my God.
jokes.punchline: Kids' horrified reaction to the TMI
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction comedy to Roland's oversharing

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, when I was a kid, my father used to take me out on the lake. We'd be out there for hours, and he'd just... let it all hang out. I mean, really let it all hang out. One time, he got so relaxed, he fell right out of the boat.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's... that's a lot.

jokes.text: Have a ball in the land down under! Ever wanna ride a kangaroo?
jokes.punchline: Random Australia references for no apparent reason
jokes.explanation: Random Roland-ism that shows his scattered thought process

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: G'day mate! I'm off to Australia to find myself. Or maybe just find a good vegemite sandwich. Either way, I'll be throwing shrimp on the barbie and wrestling crocodiles with my bare hands. Fair dinkum, as they say down under. Catch you on the flip side!

jokes.text: looks like you got all the channels except for Channel 19 for some reason. I don't know why.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific channel that doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity that's mildly funny but not particularly strong

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, we got all the channels now. Well, except for the one that's just people eating sandwiches. I've been calling about that for three years.

jokes.text: You can find it right outside the doors to your left, right beside the Hammam spa. Would you like me to book you a treatment while you're at it?
jokes.punchline: Stevie sarcastically describing non-existent amenities
jokes.explanation: Great Stevie moment - deadpan sarcasm highlighting the motel's lack of amenities

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, we have a state-of-the-art business center. It's right next to the Olympic-sized swimming pool and the spa. You can't miss it—just follow the sound of the complimentary champagne fountain.

jokes.text: Do you mind if I use your toilet?
jokes.punchline: Asking to use their bathroom instead of leaving
jokes.explanation: Roland's lack of boundaries - asking invasive favors

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Actually, before I go, can I use your bathroom?

jokes.text: Yes, that train has left the station, if you know what I mean!
jokes.punchline: Euphemism for urgent bowel movement
jokes.explanation: Roland's crude humor and TMI - in character but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I need to use the bathroom. It's urgent.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I've got to drop the kids off at the pool.

jokes.text: Because if someone were to break in here in the middle of the night wanting to murder us, they would attack this bed first. So I need this bed.
jokes.punchline: Logic that he'd use Alexis as human shield
jokes.explanation: Shows David's selfishness and paranoid thinking in humorous way

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I need the bed. In case of emergency, I'll use Alexis as a human shield.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? David, that doesn't make any sense.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, she's smaller than me, so statistically speaking, I have a better chance of survival.

jokes.text: Sort of, that was the plan, yeah.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking if he'd leave her to bleed out
jokes.punchline: David casually confirming his selfish escape plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that shows David's honest selfishness

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So you'd just leave me here to bleed out?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I would have to leave you here to bleed out. I mean, I'm not going to get on a flight covered in your blood. That seems like a lot of questions at security.

jokes.text: Like, whenever stupid Mary-Kate stops hogging his plane.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining when Stavros will pick her up
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to celebrity using boyfriend's plane
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's world where celebrities casually use her boyfriend's plane

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: He's picking me up in his plane.

jokes.text: What kind of sociopath abandons her family in some vomit-soaked dump to gallivant around the world with her dumb, shipping-heir loser boyfriend she's known for three months?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and harsh description of the situation
jokes.explanation: David's dramatic but accurate assessment of Alexis's selfishness

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You want to what? Move to another country? Without telling anyone? And start a new life with someone you met three weeks ago? Are you insane? This is not just irresponsible, Alexis, this is genuinely unhinged. You're talking about abandoning your entire family, your business obligations, and your reputation to gallivant off with some random person you barely know. It's selfish, it's reckless, it's absolutely idiotic, and frankly, it's the most Alexis thing you've ever done.

jokes.text: Um, David, it will be four months next month!
jokes.punchline: Alexis's defense that it'll be four months later
jokes.explanation: Alexis missing the point entirely - one month difference doesn't matter

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Three months? That's nothing. That's not a real relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, in a month it'll be four months, so...

jokes.text: I actually think this place is kind of cute.
jokes.punchline: Alexis finding the dump 'cute'
jokes.explanation: Alexis's delusional optimism when planning her escape

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that a dump?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's actually kind of cute.

jokes.text: Remind me to get that window fixed. It will not open.
jokes.setup: Roland emerging from lengthy bathroom visit
jokes.punchline: Suggesting window repair after stinking up bathroom
jokes.explanation: Roland's obliviousness to his own impact on others

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I think we need to get those windows fixed.

jokes.text: Oh, look! You've got Channel 19!
jokes.punchline: Channel now mysteriously works
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Channel 19 joke, adding absurdist element

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, would you look at that. Channel 19's working again.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: How is that possible? It was completely broken.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Sometimes these things just fix themselves. It's a miracle, really.

jokes.text: Roland, could you get the fuck out?
jokes.punchline: Direct profanity after trying to be polite
jokes.explanation: Johnny breaking his polite facade - effective character moment

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh, for fuck's sake!

jokes.text: There's a pharmacy worth of drugs wearing off on most of us right now
jokes.punchline: Casual admission family is coming down from drugs
jokes.explanation: Reveals the family's wealthy lifestyle included casual drug use

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I apologize for my outburst. We're all just coming down from drugs.

jokes.text: I'll just get the fuck out of here.
jokes.punchline: Roland repeating the phrase back
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Johnny's outburst - mildly funny escalation

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You're a goddamn idiot, Roland.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, you're a goddamn idiot, Johnny.

jokes.text: It is charming. It is quaint. It's like out of a storybook.
jokes.punchline: Alexis's opposite reaction to same terrible place
jokes.explanation: Alexis's delusional positivity when she thinks she's escaping

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This town is absolutely revolting. The people are unsophisticated, the infrastructure is crumbling, and I can't imagine spending another second here.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, right? But honestly, I'm kind of into it. Like, everyone here is so nice and genuine, and there's something really charming about how simple everything is. I actually feel like I belong here.

jokes.text: And I am appalled that my baby girl has turned into a selfish, duplicitous whore!
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic and harsh language for family dinner
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical overreaction using inappropriately harsh language

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You're abandoning us? Abandoning ME? After everything I've sacrificed, everything I've endured in this godforsaken town, you're just going to waltz out of here like some sort of... of... ingrate?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, it's just for the weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The weekend? The WEEKEND?! Do you have any idea what weekends mean to me? They're the only time I have to contemplate the absolute decimation of my former life while wearing my second-best caftan.

jokes.text: Oh, hello! Hi, I'm Twyla. I'll be your waitress today.
jokes.punchline: Waitress arriving at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for awkward interruption - classic sitcom beat

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You are a whore!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom!

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Can I get anyone more coffee?

jokes.text: But he is learning Spanish. No mas, le duele! I think it means, 'Stop, it hurts.'
jokes.punchline: Innocent girl casually sharing prison rape implications
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered innocently by sweet character - effective contrast

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: My cousin is in prison. He says the food is really good though. He says they have a lot of time on their hands, so they really focus on the meals. He says some of the guys have gotten really creative in the kitchen.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Twyla, that's not... those aren't cooking skills they're developing.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Oh, they're not? Well, he did mention something about being on a rotation...

jokes.text: Also, you have a big thing of dandruff on your eyebrow.
jokes.punchline: Alexis deflecting with petty personal attack
jokes.explanation: Alexis's typical deflection tactic - personal attack when cornered

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I'm sorry that I'm not content to just sit around here complaining about my life like some people. At least I'm trying to do something with myself. And another thing — your bangs are really not doing you any favors.

jokes.text: Oh, brisket!
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic exclamation before vomiting
jokes.explanation: Johnny's polite way of announcing he's about to be sick

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm not feeling well... I think I'm going to be sick.

jokes.text: There's a bumper sticker of a naked Helen Mirren.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and absurd identifying detail
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail that perfectly captures the town's weirdness

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You're looking for a house with a big sign that says 'Roland Schitt's House' on it. You can't miss it. It's the only house on the street that has a life-size cardboard cutout of Roland in the front yard wearing a tuxedo.

jokes.text: Johnny, when I was a kid and I did a bad thing, my father took the doors off my room. And he said to me, 'Roland, privacy is earned.'
jokes.punchline: Bizarre childhood punishment and misapplied life lesson
jokes.explanation: Reveals Roland's warped logic and dysfunctional upbringing

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: When I was a kid, my old man used to remove my bedroom door as punishment. Said it would teach me about privacy and consequences.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: So now, whenever I'm faced with a difficult decision, I just think: what would happen if I removed a door?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Turns out, that's not great life advice for most situations.

jokes.text: What the hell does that have to do with privacy? Nothing. I just got mad and I took your doors.
jokes.punchline: Roland admitting the explanation makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Roland's honesty about his petty revenge

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, you see, privacy is like... it's like a river, you know? And the river flows, and sometimes the water gets into your shoes, and you don't want that.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I don't know, I wasn't listening to myself.

jokes.text: I don't suppose you saw any hobos or crackheads loitering around the hotel today?
jokes.punchline: Classist assumptions about who would steal from them
jokes.explanation: Shows Moira's privileged assumptions about crime and poverty

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: My earrings are missing. Someone in this town has taken them.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, maybe you just misplaced them.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I don't misplace things, David. I'm not some sort of... regular person. It was obviously someone from the lower part of town.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, that's a really gross thing to say.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I'm simply making an observation based on socioeconomic data.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, we live in the lower part of town.

jokes.text: 'Cause I wanna hit the pawn shop before it closes. I owe my crack dealer a ton of money, so…
jokes.punchline: Stevie sarcastically playing into the stereotype
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Stevie moment - deadpan sarcasm that exposes Moira's prejudices

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, you caught me. I'm a klepto. I've been stealing from you this whole time. What was I gonna do, sell your wigs on the black market?

jokes.text: I have asked you thrice now for a towel so that I may wash this town off my body.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious language and dramatic description of basic need
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment - pretentious language for basic request

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't understand why this is so difficult. I simply require a modicum of basic service—is that really too much to ask? I mean, I'm not asking for the moon here. I'm asking for the most elementary, pedestrian level of customer service. It's not as though I'm requesting they perform neurosurgery or compose a symphony. I'm asking them to do the one thing their job description explicitly states they should do.

jokes.text: Right now, some local is pawning my earrings for crank.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic assumptions about drug trade
jokes.explanation: Moira's dramatic assumptions about small-town crime

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I don't know what you expected. I mean, if you're going to leave your purse unattended in a room full of people, you're basically asking to be robbed. It's like leaving a bag of cocaine on a nightstand in Bogotá.

jokes.text: the one thing I could hide under my tongue.
jokes.punchline: Impractical hiding place for earrings
jokes.explanation: Absurd image of Moira hiding diamonds in her mouth

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I've hidden my most valuable earrings in a place no one would ever think to look.

jokes.text: I took your earrings out of here and I put them in my shoe. For safekeeping.
jokes.punchline: Johnny's practical but gross solution
jokes.explanation: Reveals Johnny's practical thinking and anticlimactic solution

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, have you checked the drain? I once found your mother's wedding ring in the shower trap.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Johnny, that's disgusting.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, so is losing expensive jewelry. I suggest you get down there with a wire hanger and start fishing.

jokes.text: I've hated that guy ever since he asked me to do lines with him at my 60th.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of cocaine at her birthday party
jokes.explanation: Reveals more about the family's wealthy lifestyle and drug use

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, at my birthday party, Johnny mentioned that Stavros had a cocaine problem in the '80s, and I said, 'Darling, so did my eyeliner.'

jokes.text: Pick up a hammer and nail this coffin shut!
jokes.punchline: Overly theatrical metaphor for their predicament
jokes.explanation: David's dramatic overstatement typical of his character

jokes.text: remember that time when he gave me his ex-wife's engagement ring?
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning obviously inappropriate gift
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's acceptance of clearly inappropriate behavior

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: He bought me a timeshare in Turks and Caicos... without asking me. He also bought me a jet ski. A jet ski, David. Who buys someone a jet ski? And then he got me this gorgeous Birkin bag, but it was fake. A fake Birkin! Can you imagine?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's... that's actually not that bad.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh, and he also bought me a vibrator.

jokes.text: Can you do me a huge favor? And never repeat this to anyone that you respect, okay?
jokes.setup: David about to share something embarrassing
jokes.punchline: Implication that Alexis doesn't know people worth respecting
jokes.explanation: Clever insult disguised as a reasonable request - typical David

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I have something embarrassing to share.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, what?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, the implication is that you don't know any people worth respecting.

jokes.text: And never repeat this to anyone that you respect, okay?
jokes.setup: David about to share something embarrassing
jokes.punchline: His specification that Alexis can't tell people he respects
jokes.explanation: Sharp character writing - David's snobby qualification is both mean and funny

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I have something I need to tell you, but you cannot tell anyone I respect.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, that's literally everyone.

jokes.text: Of course, by then our feet will be shoeless and filthy and mangled from walking on cigarette butts and broken beer bottles.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic imagery of their future destitution
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical vision of poverty - dramatic but character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I can see our future now. We're living in a cardboard box under the overpass, and you're selling your hair to buy us a single can of beans.

jokes.text: Let's all pray we don't wake up.
jokes.punchline: Moira's death wish as bedtime blessing
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Moira's dark humor delivered as motherly blessing

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, goodnight everyone. Sleep well, and if you don't wake up, that's fine too.

tmdb_overview: After losing their fortune, the Rose family must relocate to their last remaining asset - a small town Johnny once bought as a joke.
insight: Pilot establishes absurdist tone through character quirks rather than structured setup-payoff beats.
